steps.title: Use the new evidence
steps.content: The passage first rules out wood type as the explanation for Cremonese violins' prized sound, since both modern and Cremonese violins are made from maple and spruce. The new analysis instead identifies a different factor: the older violins' wood appears to have been chemically treated by the makers.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A shifts to whether Cremonese violins were considered superior when they were made, but the passage compares them with modern instruments. Choice C returns to wood type even though the passage says modern and Cremonese violins use the same woods. Choice D claims the current and older making processes are the same, which the chemical-treatment evidence does not support. The evidence points to a treatment-based explanation for the sound. The answer is B.
